{"query":"Anthropic SpaceXAI compute resources monthly payment amount","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1758,"verdict":null,"extra_info":{"type":"featured_snippet","text":"According to documents tied to SpaceX's IPO, released in May 2026, Anthropic agreed to pay $1.25 billion per month for access to advanced AI infrastructure. The contract runs through May 2029 and could be worth more than $40 billion to SpaceX over its full term.May 21, 2026"},"results":[{"url":"https://www.cnbc.com/2026/06/05/google-to-pay-spacex-920-million-a-month-for-xai-compute-capacity.html","title":"Google to pay SpaceX $920 million a month for xAI compute capacity","content":"Ahead of a planned IPO, SpaceX has inked a deal to rent compute capacity to Google at $920 million per month for 32 months.
The agreement spans from October of this year through June 2029 at the $920 million rate, and with "capacity ramping up through September at a reduced fee."
Five years ago, Google agreed to supply computing and networking resources to SpaceX to help deliver internet service through its Starlink satellites."},{"url":"https://techcrunch.com/2026/06/05/google-will-pay-spacex-920m-per-month-for-compute/","title":"Google will pay SpaceX $920M per month for compute | TechCrunch","content":"Jun 5, 2026 — Google will pay SpaceX $920 million per month from October 2026 through June 2029 for access to “approximately 110,000 NVIDIA GPUs, CPUs, ...
Under the terms of the deal, Google will pay SpaceX $920 million per month from October 2026 through June 2029 for access to “approximately 110,000 NVIDIA GPUs, CPUs, memory, and other related components.”
Google’s access to the data center will ramp up “through September at a reduced fee,” according to the filing.
“If we fail to deliver access to the committed amount of GPUs by September 30, 2026, then following a one-month grace period, Google may immediately terminate the agreement or accept the number of GPUs provided” with a reduction in the monthly fees, it reads.
Its stake in Musk’s company is expected to be worth more than $100 billion after the IPO."},{"url":"https://www.basenor.com/blogs/news/anthropic-will-pay-spacex-1-25b-month-for-ai-compute","title":"Anthropic Will Pay SpaceX $1.25B/Month for AI Compute","content":"Anthropic has agreed to pay SpaceX $1.25 billion per month for AI compute capacity through May 2029 — a contract that could generate more than $40 billion in total revenue for the Elon Musk-linked company.
The compute will be used for inference workloads and to expand capacity for Claude Pro and Claude Max subscribers — directly raising usage limits on Claude Code and the Claude API.
Prior to this agreement, Colossus 1 was reportedly running at roughly 11% utilization by xAI — a staggering amount of idle GPU capacity sitting in Memphis. xAI had already migrated its primary training operations to Colossus 2, leaving Colossus 1 as an expensive asset generating little return."},{"url":"https://www.linkedin.com/pulse/anthropic-paying-125-billion-per-month-access-wclsc","title":"Anthropic Is Paying $1.25 Billion Per Month To Access SpaceX’s Compute Capacity, & More","content":"According to Caplight, nearly 40% of SPVs investing in SpaceX and Anduril over the past three years are charging annual fees, including management fees averaging 1.2-1.5% and carried interest at 13-15%.
According to our research, slowing biological aging by 5–15% across the current US population could generate roughly $10–30 trillion in total incremental value, the largest share accruing to younger people as the benefits compound over their remaining years of life."},{"url":"https://chamath.substack.com/p/what-i-read-this-week-183","title":"SpaceX and Anthropic 300MW Compute Partnership","content":"The facility gives Anthropic access to more than 300 megawatts of capacity and over 220,000 NVIDIA GPUs, including H100s, H200s, and GB200s.
New Street Research estimates the contract will generate $3-$4 billion in annual revenue for SpaceX, with more than $2.5 billion in cash profit.
The raise comes two weeks after DeepSeek released V4 on April 24, a 1.6-trillion-parameter open-weight model whose Pro variant scored slightly higher than GPT-5.4’s model in Codeforces and matching Opus 4.6 Max on SWE-bench Verified."}]}

The deal, announced around May 6 and confirmed by multiple sources this week, hands Anthropic full access to xAI's Colossus 1 data center in Memphis, Tennessee, while xAI shifts its own training workloads to the newer Colossus 2 facility. Anthropic is ramping to 100% utilization of that capacity within May 2026, with a discounted rate applying through June 2026 before full pricing kicks in. The deal, the company said, “allows us to monetize unused compute capacity in our infrastructure.” The terms of the deal allow either side to terminate the contract with 90 days’ notice.
